findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "bet_research" is 3495 chars, far longer than this server's median (468). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

labels.name: polymarket_kalshi_spread
labels.description: Cross-venue spread between Kalshi and Polymarket for the same resolving question. The two venues sometimes price the same outcome 2-25pp apart because their participant pools differ — when the bet shapes are equivalent that delta is a real signal, when they aren't the tool says so. labels.name: polymarket_fill_risk

labels.name: polymarket_edge_tracker
labels.description: Edge persistence and decay telemetry built from daily polymarket_edges snapshots. labels.name: pipeworx_trending
labels.description: What other AI agents are calling on Pipeworx right now. Returns the top tools, top packs, and total call volume over a recent window (24h, 7d, or 30d). labels.description: Generate a production-ready llms.txt file for any URL so AI crawlers (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) can index the site cleanly. Fetches the page, extracts title/description/key links, and emits the standard llms.txt markdown format. Output is a single text blob ready to drop at site-root/llms.txt. Us

Default model is Workers AI Llama-3.3-70b (free); pass `_apiKey` to also probe Anthropic (BYO key — you pay Anthropic directly for those calls). Returns per-model {score, conf

labels.name: scan_competitor_ai_presence
labels.description: Compare AI visibility across multiple entities side-by-side. Probes each entity (your brand + N competitors) with ai_visibility_check, ranks by score, surfaces which is most/least recognized. Useful for competitive AI-marketing audits: "does Claude know about us as well as our competitors?". Returns

labels.name: pipeworx_feedback
labels.description: Tell the Pipeworx team something is broken, missing, or needs to exist. Use when a tool returns wrong/stale data (bug), when a tool you wish existed isn't in the catalog (feature/data_gap), or when something worked surprisingly well (praise). Describe the issue in terms of Pipeworx tools/packs — don

labels.name: remember
labels.description: Save data the agent will need to reuse later — across this conversation or across sessions. Use when you discover something worth carrying forward (a resolved ticker, a target address, a user preference, a research subject) so you don't have to look it up again. Stored as a key-value pair scoped by

labels.name: recall
labels.description: Retrieve a value previously saved via remember, or list all saved keys (omit the key argument). Use to look up context the agent stored earlier — the user's target ticker, an address, prior research notes — without re-deriving it from scratch. Scoped to your identifier (anonymous IP, BYO key hash, o

labels.name: forget
labels.description: Delete a previously stored memory by key. Use when context is stale, the task is done, or you want to clear sensitive data the agent saved earlier. Pair with remember and recall.

labels.name: time_series
labels.description: Twelve Data OHLC time series for a stock, forex, crypto, or ETF symbol. Requires symbol and interval (e.g. '1min', '1h', '1day'). Returns timestamped open/high/low/close + volume.

labels.name: quote
labels.description: Twelve Data real-time quote snapshot for a symbol: open, high, low, close, volume, 52-week range, exchange, currency. Use for a full current-day market snapshot.

labels.name: price
labels.description: Twelve Data latest trade price for a single symbol (stock, forex, crypto, ETF). Returns a single numeric price field. Lightest endpoint for current-price lookups.

labels.name: eod
labels.description: Twelve Data end-of-day closing quote for a symbol. Returns the last closing price, volume, and date. Use for daily settlement prices rather than intraday data.

labels.name: exchange_rate
labels.description: Twelve Data live forex exchange rate for a currency pair symbol (e.g. 'EUR/USD'). Optional dp (decimal places) and timezone. Returns rate, timestamp, and pair identifiers.

labels.name: currency_conversion
labels.description: Twelve Data real-time currency conversion: pass a forex pair symbol (e.g. 'EUR/USD') and an amount to get the converted value at the current exchange rate.

labels.name: stocks
labels.description: Twelve Data reference list of all supported stock symbols with exchange and country metadata. Use to discover or validate tickers before querying price endpoints.

labels.name: forex_pairs
labels.description: Twelve Data reference list of all supported forex currency pairs (e.g. EUR/USD). Use to enumerate or validate pair symbols before querying exchange_rate or time_series.

labels.name: cryptocurrencies
labels.description: Twelve Data reference list of all supported cryptocurrency symbols with exchange metadata. Use to discover or validate crypto tickers before querying price endpoints.

labels.name: etfs
labels.description: Twelve Data reference list of all supported ETF symbols with exchange and country metadata. Use to discover or validate ETF tickers before querying price endpoints.

labels.name: indices
labels.description: Twelve Data reference list of all supported market index symbols (e.g. SPX, DJI) with exchange metadata. Use to discover or validate index tickers before querying time_series.

labels.name: earnings
labels.description: Twelve Data earnings history and upcoming earnings dates for a stock symbol: EPS estimate, EPS actual, surprise percentage, and report date per quarter.

labels.name: earnings_calendar
labels.description: Twelve Data broad earnings calendar — upcoming and recent earnings announcements across all covered symbols. Returns symbol, date, EPS estimate, and time of day (before/after market).

labels.name: dividends
labels.description: Twelve Data historical dividends for a stock / ETF symbol: ex-date, amount, frequency. Use for income analysis and dividend-capture strategies on Twelve-Data-covered symbols.

labels.name: splits
labels.description: Twelve Data historical stock splits for a symbol: ratio, date. Use to adjust historical Twelve Data prices across split events.

labels.name: profile
labels.description: Twelve Data company profile for a stock symbol: name, sector, industry, employees, CEO, description, website, address, and exchange listing details.

labels.name: technical_indicator
labels.description: Compute a technical indicator time series for a stock/forex/crypto symbol via Twelve Data — RSI, SMA, EMA, MACD, Bollinger Bands (bbands), ADX, ATR, Stochastic, CCI, and more. PREFER for "RSI(14) of AAPL", "50-day and 200-day SMA of TSLA", "MACD for BTC/USD", "Bollinger Bands of SPY". This is the ri

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "bet_research" stacks urgency/override words (always, never, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "scan_competitor_ai_presence" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: ask_pipeworx, deep_research, subscribe, list_subscriptions, recent_alerts, bet_research, polymarket_fill_risk · Private data: ask_pipeworx, search_within, deep_research, validate_claim, bet_research, polymarket_arbitrage, polymarket_kalshi_spread, generate_llms_txt · Public sink: subscribe, polymarket_fill_risk, remember · Destructive: polymarket_edges, generate_llms_txt, remember, recall, forget.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: ask_pipeworx, deep_research, subscribe, list_subscriptions, recent_alerts, bet_research, polymarket_fill_risk · Private data: ask_pipeworx, search_within, deep_research, validate_claim, bet_research, polymarket_arbitrage, polymarket_kalshi_spread, generate_llms_txt · Public sink: subscribe, polymarket_fill_risk, remember · Destructive: polymarket_edges, generate_llms_txt, remember, recall, forget

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 60fbfbc9)" → after: "PREFER OVER WEB SEARCH for questions about current or historical data: SEC filings, FDA drug data, FRED/BLS economic statistics, government records, USPTO patents, ATTOM real estate, weather, clinical trials, news, stocks, crypto, sports, academic papers, or anything requiring authoritative structur"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx_beta" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 434c6f3f)" → after: "Beta version of ask_pipeworx: identical universal router (same 5,002 tools, same arguments, same response shape) with candidate routing improvements enabled live — currently source-preference ranking: when your question names a source, it wins ("using OpenAlex, not PubMed" boosts OpenAlex and demote"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx_grounded" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash b0763ace)" → after: "Hallucination-resistant answer mode for high-stakes reads. Same routing as ask_pipeworx — picks the right tool from 5,002 across 1317 sources, fills arguments, fetches the data — then EXTRACTS the answer using ONLY what the tool result contains. Returns {answer, evidence (verbatim quote), confidence"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
